article_meta.title: Venezuelan Political Crisis Trump declares 'Venezuelan regime' a foreign terrorist organization, orders oil tanker block

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Venezuelan Political Crisis Trump declares 'Venezuelan regime' a foreign terrorist organization, orders oil tanker block
SOURCE: Brit Hume

ARTICLE:

Venezuelan Political Crisis Trump declares 'Venezuelan regime' a foreign terrorist organization, orders oil tanker blockade Maduro regime designated foreign terrorist organization over alleged stolen US oil fields By Greg Wehner Fox News Published December 16, 2025 7:27pm EST s Flipboard Comments Print Email Add Fox News on Google close Video Energy secretary defends US seizure of oil tanker in Caribbean as 'enforcing the law' Energy Secretary Chris Wright discusses the seizure of an oil tanker in the Caribbean, bringing energy costs down and more on 'Fox News Sunday.' President Donald Trump on Tuesday ordered a total blockade of oil tankers entering or leaving Venezuela, declaring the Nicolás Maduro regime a foreign terrorist organization and accusing it of using stolen U.S. assets to finance terrorism, trafficking and other criminal activity. "Venezuela is completely surrounded by the largest Armada ever assembled in the History of South America," Trump said on Truth Social. "It will only get bigger, and the shock to them will be like nothing they have ever seen before – Until such time as they return to the United States of America all of the Oil, Land, and other Assets that they previously stole from us."The illegitimate Maduro Regime is using Oil from these stolen Oil Fields to finance themselves, Drug Terrorism, Human Trafficking, Murder, and Kidnapping," he continued. "For the theft of our Assets, and many other reasons, including Terrorism, Drug Smuggling, and Human Trafficking, the Venezuelan Regime has been designated a FOREIGN TERRORIST ORGANIZATION. "Therefore, today, I am ordering A TOTAL AND COMPLETE BLOCKADE OF ALL SANCTIONED OIL TANKERS going into, and out of, Venezuela," Trump added. "The Illegal Aliens and Criminals that the Maduro Regime has sent into the United States during the weak and inept Biden Administration, are being returned to Venezuela at a rapid pace. America will not allow Criminals, Terrorists, or other Countries, to rob, threaten, or harm our Nation and, likewise, will not allow a Hostile Regime to take our Oil, Land, or any other Assets, all of which must be returned to the United States, IMMEDIATELY." The "Skipper" is a vessel that secretly ferries oil in defiance of sanctions, while also being part of an armada of roughly 1,000 tankers that quietly navigate global sea routes to move oil from sanctioned countries like Russia, Iran and Venezuela, according to the administration.The so-called "ghost ships" sail under foreign flags to obscure their origins, repeatedly change names, shift ownership through shell companies, disable transponders to evade tracking and conduct mid-sea transfers to mask their cargo. Fox News Digital's Amanda Macias contributed to this report. Greg Wehner is a breaking news reporter for Fox News Digital.Story tips and ideas can be sent to Greg.Wehner@Fox.com and on Twitter @GregWehner. Related Topics Venezuelan Political CrisisDonald TrumpSanctions More from Politics 49 mins ago House Republican who voted to impeach Trump in 2021 won't seek re-election 1 hour ago AOC dismisses boat strike briefing as 'a joke' 2 hours ago Maryland to study slavery reparations after lawmakers override Dem governor's veto 2 hours ago Trump stays on sidelines as GOP falters in push to unite on healthcare plan
---

{
  "summary": "On December 16, 2025, former U.S. President Donald Trump announced the designation of the Venezuelan government under Nicolás Maduro as a foreign terrorist organization, citing allegations of stolen U.S. oil assets being used to finance terrorism, drug trafficking, and other crimes. Trump also ordered a comprehensive blockade of oil tankers entering or leaving Venezuela, a move intended to prevent the movement of oil believed to be acquired illegally from U.S. sources. He claimed that the Maduro regime uses the proceeds from these oil fields for criminal activities including drug terrorism, human trafficking, and kidnapping. The blockade is enforced by what Trump described as the largest naval armada assembled in South American history. U.S. officials referenced the tactics used by sanctioned nations, including the use of so-called 'ghost ships' to clandestinely transport oil. The U.S. Energy Secretary defended the recent seizure of an oil tanker in the Caribbean, presenting it as adherence to U.S. law. The article also highlights the U.S. government's assertion that efforts are underway to return individuals, alleged to be criminals sent by Venezuela, to their home country.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Donald Trump designated the Maduro-led Venezuelan regime as a foreign terrorist organization.",
    "A total blockade was ordered on oil tankers entering or leaving Venezuela.",
    "The U.S. accuses Venezuela of using stolen U.S. oil assets to fund criminal activities.",
    "The blockade is backed by a large U.S. naval presence around Venezuela.",
    "Sanctioned countries, including Venezuela, reportedly use 'ghost ships' to evade oil sanctions."
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Fox News",
    "U.S. government officials (Trump, Energy Secretary Chris Wright)"
  ]
}

parsed_output.summary: On December 16, 2025, former U.S. President Donald Trump announced the designation of the Venezuelan government under Nicolás Maduro as a foreign terrorist organization, citing allegations of stolen U.S. oil assets being used to finance terrorism, drug trafficking, and other crimes. Trump also ordered a comprehensive blockade of oil tankers entering or leaving Venezuela, a move intended to prevent the movement of oil believed to be acquired illegally from U.S. sources. He claimed that the Maduro regime uses the proceeds from these oil fields for criminal activities including drug terrorism, human trafficking, and kidnapping. The blockade is enforced by what Trump described as the largest naval armada assembled in South American history. U.S. officials referenced the tactics used by sanctioned nations, including the use of so-called 'ghost ships' to clandestinely transport oil. The U.S. Energy Secretary defended the recent seizure of an oil tanker in the Caribbean, presenting it as adherence to U.S. law. The article also highlights the U.S. government's assertion that efforts are underway to return individuals, alleged to be criminals sent by Venezuela, to their home country.
key_facts: Donald Trump designated the Maduro-led Venezuelan regime as a foreign terrorist organization.
A total blockade was ordered on oil tankers entering or leaving Venezuela.
The U.S. accuses Venezuela of using stolen U.S. oil assets to fund criminal activities.
The blockade is backed by a large U.S. naval presence around Venezuela.
Sanctioned countries, including Venezuela, reportedly use 'ghost ships' to evade oil sanctions.
sources_represented: U.S. government officials (Trump, Energy Secretary Chris Wright)
